title: Spiloxene umbraticola
taxonomy: Spiloxene umbraticola is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypoxidaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Southern Africa, particularly in South Africa and Lesotho. This plant is a type of cormous perennial herb, which means it has underground storage organs called corms. The classification of Spiloxene umbraticola is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Asparagales. The plant's classification is an essential aspect of understanding its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Spiloxene umbraticola, commonly known as the umbrella pincushion, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Cape Province of South Africa, belonging to the family Iridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Spiloxene umbraticola include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spiloxene umbraticola is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for skin and wound healing.
